questions.question: Should large test files be split by describe group or by test count?
questions.recommendation: B: Split by describe group so related tests stay together
questions.rationale: Describe groups represent coherent features; splitting by count would scatter related tests

questions.question: How should access-tracking testability seam be exposed?
questions.recommendation: B: Change return type to Promise<void> so tests can await directly
questions.rationale: Returning the promise is backward-compatible with all existing callers and requires no middleware changes

discovery.summary: All questions answered — proceeding with split-by-describe-group and Promise<void> return type approaches.

parallel_analysis.note: Waves must run sequentially for verification. Within Wave 1, T-01 through T-05 are independent. Within Wave 4, T-09/T-10 target different files. Wave 5 splits are independent.

checkpoint_fallback.reason: Diff artifact (181KB) exceeds 150KB hard cap due to large test file splits in Wave 5. Manual verification: all 1232 web tests pass, TypeScript builds clean, YAML is valid. Test file changes are structural only (split by describe group) with no production logic changes.
